# Support

BlazorDX is **free and open source (MIT)** — the entire library, forever. There are no
license keys and nothing in the code is gated behind a paid tier. The paid tiers below fund
continued development and serve teams that need guarantees the community channel can't
provide; they buy *people's time and commitments*, not access to the code.

## Community — free

The default for everyone.

- **Questions & help:** ask in [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/logixrcorp/BlazorDX/discussions) (the **Q&A** category). Share ideas in **Ideas**, or what you built in **Show and tell**.
- **Bugs:** open an [issue](https://github.com/logixrcorp/BlazorDX/issues) with a minimal reproduction.
- **Docs:** the in-app documentation site (`/docs` in the demo) and the guides in this repo.
- Best-effort, no response-time guarantee. Bug reports with a minimal reproduction get the
  fastest attention.

## Priority support — paid

For teams that need a dependable response.

- Private support channel with a target **response time** (e.g. 1–2 business days).
- Help with upgrades, integration, and configuration.
- Triage and prioritization of bugs that block you.
- Contact: **support@blazordx.com**.

## Enterprise / SLA — paid

For production-critical adoption and procurement.

- Contractual **SLA** on response and fix targets.
- Help passing your **accessibility (WCAG/VPAT)** and **security** reviews.
- **IP indemnification** and a signed support agreement.
- Influence over the roadmap and early access to releases.
- Contact: **support@blazordx.com**.

## Custom development & consulting

- Bespoke components, theming, or integrations built to spec.
- Architecture review, training, and onboarding for your team.
- Contact: **support@blazordx.com**.

---

The code stays MIT regardless of tier. Paying for support never removes anything from the
open-source library — it adds people, guarantees, and time. Security reports go to
**security@blazordx.com** — see [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md).
